signals: Leadership expects faster delivery due to AI tooling
Routine implementation perceived as less scarce
More pressure to guide technical direction

workflow_change.before: Balanced architecture work with substantial hands-on coding and mentoring.
workflow_change.after: Still codes, but more time goes to defining patterns, reviewing AI-assisted output, and making boundary decisions for systems.

actions: Created guidance for responsible AI-assisted development
Spent more time mentoring juniors on validation and trade-offs

outcome.early_effect: Role remains strong, but leverage now depends more on judgment than output volume.

narrative.summary: Seniority feels more valuable when it translates into system judgment. AI seems to reduce the scarcity of routine coding more than the scarcity of architectural thinking.
